# function: validate_netcidr

Accepts a string and returns `true` if it is a valid IPv4 CIDR notation AND the address has no host bits set (i.e. it is a proper network address). For example, `"11.12.13.0/24"` returns `true`, but `"11.12.13.0/16"` returns `false` because the `.13.0` portion lies outside the /16 network boundary.

## Example Usage

```terraform
output "map_product" {
  value = provider::functions::validate_netcidr("10.1.1.0/24")
}
```

## Signature

<!-- signature generated by tfplugindocs -->
```text
validate_netcidr(cidr string) bool
```

## Arguments

<!-- arguments generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `cidr` (String) The string to validate as an IPv4 network CIDR notation.
